title: uragoga medians
parts: Uragoga medians has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, which are used to treat various health issues and contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and saponins. The leaves of the Uragoga medians plant are also utilized in traditional medicine and contain constituents like alkaloids and terpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers and stems of the plant are used to treat fever and contain medicinally active compounds like glycosides and phenolic acids. Additionally, the bark of the Uragoga medians plant is used to treat various skin conditions and contains constituents like tannins and phenolic compounds, which have astringent and antioxidant properties.
side_effects: Uragoga medians can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising. In severe cases, improper use of uragoga medians can cause seizures, coma, and even death. Additionally, improper dosing can interact with other medications, exacerbating side effects and reducing the plant's therapeutic benefits.
